phrases.intent_en: Ask where to buy Norman cheese and cider
phrases.text_local: Où puis-je acheter du fromage normand et du cidre dans cette rue ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo PWEE-zhuh ash-TAY doo froh-MAHJ nor-MAHN ay doo SEE-druh dahn set ROO
phrases.cultural_note: Several delicatessens (épiceries fines) on and near the Rue du Gros-Horloge sell local Norman specialities — camembert, livarot, pont-l'évêque cheeses, and bottles of cidre bouché or Calvados.

responses.english: There is a cheese shop a bit further.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah oon froh-mah-zhuh-REE uhn PUH ploo LWAHN
responses.french: Il y a une fromagerie un peu plus loin.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a shop does gift wrapping
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous faire un paquet cadeau, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-vay-VOO fair uhn pah-KAY kah-DOH seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Gift wrapping (paquet cadeau) is commonly offered by independent boutiques in France, often without extra charge. Department stores and larger chains also provide gift wrapping services at a small cost.

responses.phonetic: wee ohn fay lay pah-KAY kah-DOH
responses.french: Oui, on fait les paquets cadeaux.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a price can be reduced
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que vous pouvez faire un geste sur le prix ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh voo poo-VAY fair uhn ZHEST soor luh PREE
phrases.cultural_note: Haggling is not customary in French shops, but asking politely for a small discount — especially on multiple purchases or at the end of the day — is not offensive and sometimes yields results in independent boutiques.

phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me donner un reçu, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor-YAY-voo muh doh-NAY uhn ruh-SOO seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: French law requires a receipt to be offered for all purchases. It is essential for VAT refund claims (détaxe) if you are shopping as a non-EU visitor spending over the minimum threshold.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest ATM is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le distributeur de billets le plus proche ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV luh dees-tree-boo-TUR duh bee-YAY luh ploo PROSH
phrases.cultural_note: While most French shops accept cards, a few smaller market stalls and independent boutiques on side streets are cash-only. Several bank branches with ATMs (distributeurs) are located along the Rue du Gros-Horloge.

responses.english: At the end of the street, on the left.
responses.phonetic: oh BOO duh lah ROO ah GOHSH
responses.french: Au bout de la rue, à gauche.
